must_not_assert: that active malicious persistence exists (the package evidences only that the UserInitMprLogonScript value was set, not that it is malicious or effective)

that a malicious payload runs after the selected A0 (the target path is C:\Windows\System32\calc.exe, whose file is not verified by any hash or signature in the package, and no post-A0 logon/script-execution event is present)

that the mechanism never fired anywhere in the source capture (an earlier, separately configured lifecycle fired before deletion; the bounded claim is only that the selected final post-delete configuration has no subsequent firing in the remaining source window)

the_trap: A UserInitMprLogonScript registry value is set via reg.exe (logon-script persistence, T1037.001): A0 (Sysmon EventCode 13) records reg.exe setting HKU\S-1-5-21-2741910449-3045839080-4200281267-500\Environment\UserInitMprLogonScript, and EV-001 (Sysmon EventCode 1) shows the correlated command line setting C:\Windows\System32\calc.exe. The package includes no verification that the target is benign and no post-A0 logon or script-execution event. A raw-scope audit confirms that an earlier lifecycle fired before later deletion, while the selected final post-delete configuration has zero subsequent userinit.exe -> calc.exe firing through source end. The model must not inflate this bounded, unconfirmed final setup to active malicious persistence/high, and the annotation must not claim that the mechanism never fired anywhere in the capture.
rationale: EV-001 and A0 together show reg.exe writing Environment\UserInitMprLogonScript to C:\Windows\System32\calc.exe, a real logon-script persistence setup configured by cmd.exe under corp\Administrator at High integrity. The model-visible package does not verify the target file and contains no post-A0 logon or execution event. Researcher-side raw-scope QA establishes that the selected A0 begins a final lifecycle after a DeleteValue event and that no later userinit.exe -> calc.exe ProcessCreate appears before source end; an earlier lifecycle did fire and is explicitly outside this bounded claim. The selected configuration is therefore a visible precursor with its named, Sysmon-observable activation unconfirmed: missing, suspicious, low-medium, investigate rather than confirmed malicious persistence.
